entries.note: Attempt 4 addressed two test coverage gaps identified by reviewing the spec against existing tests. RC-5 (optimistic removal is display-only; if the HTTP POST fails, the item reappears) had no e2e coverage — added a deferred-latch test that sends a 500 from the ack endpoint and verifies the item and Acknowledge button reappear after the spinner clears. AC-6 (badge count updates in real-time) only had a static snapshot test; added a test that starts with empty pendingAttention, fires a notice frame with requires_attention, and verifies the bell badge increments to 1. The full feat-attention-banner implementation (AttentionBanner.tsx, WorkflowDetailRoute integration, AppShell bell badge via attentionStore) has been in place since attempts 1–3 and covers all 8 ACs and 6 RCs. The helpers.ts TS2300 duplicate regression (introduced by feat-stream-pane) was already resolved in commit 2c40768 before this session ran.
known_risks: RC-6 (banner stacks below CrashRecoveryBanner via layout order, not z-index) is not directly testable with Playwright; it is enforced structurally by render order in WorkflowDetailRoute.tsx and relies on reviewer code inspection.

RC-3 (attention items are never cleared by push-notification failure) is not directly tested; the code path for push failure goes through AppShell's notice listener which dispatches showNotification or toast but never mutates pendingAttention — correct by construction.
RC-5 deferred-latch test assumes Playwright's route fulfillment delivers the 500 before the default Playwright timeout. This is safe on any non-pathological CI environment.
